summary: Problem 6 (Spielman) asks whether a universal constant c exists for ε-light subsets, conjectured tight at c=1/2 — an open research-level question with an 18% solvability estimate. The current best proof (claude_opus_5-4) carries a contradictory status: all 16 tracked issues are marked resolved at the question level, yet the proof record shows '100 open issues' and the experiment field is unset, indicating the proof has NOT actually closed despite extensive barrier-method scaffolding. The resolved issues cluster around making the barrier/interlacing argument (Tr_sigma symmetrization, Ky Fan principles, Laplacian PSD orderings) rigorous, but the core existence/tightness of the universal constant remains the unresolved mathematical blocker.
problems: Status inconsistency: 16 issues marked 'resolved' but best-proof record reports '100 open issues' and an unset experiment field, so the proof is not verified complete.

Core blocker is the existence of a universal constant c (conjectured 1/2) for ε-light subsets — none of the 16 resolved issues directly establish this central claim; they only repair the supporting barrier machinery.

Heavy reliance on a delicate barrier/potential-function argument (Tr_sigma, M^p decomposition, Ky Fan max + subadditivity) that required many sequential patches, suggesting the argument's overall coherence may still be fragile after local fixes.
Greedy Lemma (lem:greedy) and Barrier Update Lemma (lem:bu) were repeatedly reworked; their interaction and the rank<=sigma / leverage-invariant base case were the most error-prone components.
18% solvability indicates low confidence that any current line of attack yields a complete, tight proof of the conjecture.
highlights: All 16 individually tracked gaps have documented resolutions with concrete fixes (symmetrized Tr_sigma convention, eliminated undefined negative pseudo-power, separated Ky Fan maximum vs subadditivity, proved L_{S,V\S} ⪯ L).
Foundational reductions were strengthened beyond the original scope: ker L ⊆ ker L_S equivalence and Foster's identity / rank formula now handle disconnected G, not just connected graphs.
Barrier Update Lemma was rewritten into 8 explicit verifiable steps and the Greedy Lemma now has a dedicated formal proof connecting it to lem:bu, improving auditability.
The leverage-invariant base case for an arbitrary initial vertex was identified and fixed, removing a previously CRITICAL flaw.

suggested_todos.title: Reconcile the '100 open issues' best-proof status against the 16 resolved-issue tracker to determine the true current state of the proof
Verify end-to-end that the patched barrier argument actually yields a universal constant c and quantify the constant obtained vs the conjectured c=1/2
Run a full re-verification pass over the assembled lem:greedy and lem:bu proofs to confirm local fixes compose into a globally valid argument
Document explicitly which step (if any) currently establishes existence of the universal constant, isolating the remaining genuine mathematical gap from already-resolved technical gaps
Set/record the experiment field and model provenance for the best-proof artifact to remove metadata ambiguity
Stress-test the tightness claim (c=1/2) with small explicit graph constructions to gather evidence on whether the conjectured bound is achievable
